category: Respiratory | GI | Neonatal | Infectious | ENT | Dermatology | Urology | General | Emergency

first_line_drugs.notes: Dose in mg/kg/dose or mg/kg/day, frequency, route, duration (e.g., 15 mg/kg/dose q6h PO, max 60 mg/kg/day)

second_line_drugs.notes: Dose, frequency, route, when to use (e.g., if first-line fails after 48-72 hours)

investigations.name: Investigation name (e.g., CBC, Chest X-ray, CRP)
investigations.indication: When to order (e.g., Persistent fever >3 days, suspected bacterial infection)

counselling: Point 1: Key message for parents in simple language
warning_signs: Diagnosis-specific warning sign 2 (e.g., Unable to drink)
referral_criteria: When to refer to specialist (e.g., Refer to pediatric pulmonologist if no improvement in 48-72 hours)
hospitalisation_criteria: When to admit (e.g., SpO2 <92%, unable to feed, toxic appearance, age <2 months)
notes: Brief clinical notes (e.g., Viral illness, self-limiting 5-7 days, no routine antibiotics)
source: Guideline references (e.g., IAP 2024, WHO 2023, GINA 2024, AAP 2023)
guideline_changes: Any recent 2024-2025 guideline updates (or null if none)
expected_course: Timeline of illness (e.g., Fever: 2-3 days. Cough: 5-7 days. Full recovery: 7-10 days.)
key_clinical_points: Clinical pearl 1 (e.g., Lactulose = cornerstone of treatment)

severity_assessment.mild: Description of mild presentation (e.g., Occasional cough, no distress)
severity_assessment.moderate: Description of moderate (e.g., Tachypnea, mild retractions)
severity_assessment.severe: Description of severe (e.g., Marked distress, hypoxia, poor feeding)
